A BRIEF EF1 INTENSITY TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN COVENTRY TOWNSHIP IN SUMMIT COUNTY, OH AT 616 PM EDT. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SIGNS AND A LIGHT POLE AT A BURGER KING ON MANCHESTER ROAD. TWO CARS WERE FLIPPED OVER OR SPUN AROUND IN THE PARKING AREA ACROSS THE ROAD NEAR WENDYS.

SHERIFF REPORTED NUMEROUS ROAD CLOSURES FROM PORT CLINTON TO OAK HARBOR. BASEMENTS HAVE BEEN FLOODED AND TWO FAMILIES HAVE BEEN DISPLACED FROM THEIR HOME. NUMEROUS ROAD CLOSURES AND STANDING WATER HAVE PERSISTED THROUGH THE OVERNIGHT HOURS INTO THE MORNING AND HAS BEEN REPORTED FROM THE MEDIA AND PUBLIC. A STATE OF EMERGENCY HAS BEEN DECLARED FOR PORT CLINTON AND OAK HARBOR DUE TO THE FLOODING.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED A WEAK EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR OREGON OHIO ON THE EVENING OF JULY 31ST AND TRACKED NORTH TO NEAR POINT PLACE BEFORE ISSIPATING. THE 5 MILE INTERMITTENT TRACK PRODUCED MAINLY TREE DAMAGE. A FEW BUILDINGS AND HOMES WERE HIT IN THE PATH. THE DAMAGE TO HOMES CONSISTED OF MAINLY SHINGLES AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. A BARN DID SUSTAIN SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE. REPORTS WERE ALSO RECEIVED OF CARS BEING DISPLACED. THE ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE UP TO 85 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 25 YARDS AND AN INTERMITTENT PATH LENGTH OF 5 MILES.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR CONNEAUTVILLE AND TRACKED EASTWARD NEAR HIGHWAY 198. THE TORNADO HEAVILY DAMAGED A DUPLEX AND NURSING HOME. THE TORNADO KNOCKED DOWN AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES IN THE AREA. THE TORNADO ALSO DAMAGED SEVERAL OTHER HOMES AND STRUCTURES BEFORE LIFTING. THIS STORM SURVEY IS NOT COMPLETE AND WILL RESUME WEDNESDAY WITH ADDITIONAL TORNADO SURVEYS EXPECTED FURTHER EAST INTO CRAWFORD COUNTY. AN UPDATED PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT ON THIS TORNADO WILL BE SENT OUT TOMORROW. THE ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE UP TO 115 MPH WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS.
CONFIRMED TORNADO. NURSING HOME STRUCK BY TORNADO.
SNAPCHAT VIDEO OF SEVERAL TREES DOWN AT THE FOUNTAIN HOUSE CORNERS AREA AT 198 AND 86.
ON ROUTE 86 BETWEEN CAMBRIDGE SPRINGS AND WOODCOCK.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR PRICE ROAD IN RURAL CRAWFORD COUNTY JUST NORTH OF BLOOMING VALLEY, PENNSYLVANIA. THE TORNADO TRACKED EAST-SOUTHEAST TOWARDS HIGHWAY 77 AND THE RICHMOND TOWNSHIP AREA. THERE WERE NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH IN A HEAVILY FOREST AREA. AT LEAST ONE HOME HAD MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF AND EXTERIOR DAMAGE ALONG OREGON CORNERS ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR NORTH RICHMOND ROAD AND HIGHWAY 77. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH AROUND 3.5 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 75 YARDS. THE ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS WERE UP TO 105 MPH.
